card.intuition: Split data into k folds; train on k-1 folds and evaluate on the held-out fold, rotating which fold is held out. Average the k validation scores for an unbiased estimate of generalisation error. More reliable than a single train/val split.
card.analogy: Grading students by letting each one serve as the test case once while the others study together. The average score across all 'test' rounds gives a fair estimate of how the class performs overall — no single student's result is the whole story.
card.time_complexity: O(k · T_train) where T_train = time to train on n·(k-1)/k samples
when_to_use: Hyperparameter tuning — use k-fold to evaluate each hyperparameter setting
Model selection — compare different model families with fair evaluation
Small datasets where a single held-out split wastes too much training data
gotchas: Shuffle before splitting — especially for time-series data where shuffling is inappropriate (use time-series CV instead)
Stratified k-fold preserves class proportions in each fold — important for imbalanced datasets
Nested CV is needed for unbiased hyperparameter tuning AND test evaluation

exercise.prompt: Implement k-fold cross-validation and return per-fold validation scores.
exercise.starter_code:
def k_fold_cv(X, y, model_fn, score_fn, k=5, shuffle=True, seed=42):
    """
    model_fn : callable(X_train, y_train) -> model
    score_fn : callable(model, X_val, y_val) -> float
    Returns list of k validation scores.
    """
    import random
    random.seed(seed)
    n       = len(X)
    indices = list(range(n))
    if shuffle: random.shuffle(indices)
    fold_size = n // k
    scores    = []

    for i in range(k):
        val_idx   = indices[i * fold_size : (i+1) * fold_size]
        train_idx = indices[:i*fold_size] + ___________
        X_val  = [X[j] for j in val_idx];   y_val  = [y[j] for j in val_idx]
        X_tr   = [X[j] for j in train_idx]; y_tr   = [y[j] for j in train_idx]
        model  = model_fn(X_tr, y_tr)
        scores.append(score_fn(model, X_val, y_val))
    return scores
hints: The training indices are everything except the current validation fold: concat the part before fold i and the part after fold i
exercise.test_code:
# Simple model: predict mean of training y
def train(X, y): return sum(y)/len(y)
def score(model, X_val, y_val):
    preds = [model]*len(y_val)
    return sum((p-y)**2 for p,y in zip(preds,y_val))/len(y_val)

X = [[i] for i in range(20)]
y = [float(i) for i in range(20)]
scores = k_fold_cv(X, y, train, score, k=4)
assert len(scores) == 4
assert all(s > 0 for s in scores)
# Scores should be similar across folds (data is regular)
assert max(scores) / min(scores) < 5
print('PASS')
